vision_report.compositional_truth_check: The registry is largely inaccurate regarding the current biological state of the specimens. P1 (Yellow Pot) is devoid of visible foliage. P2/P4 (Black Pot) contains a single, struggling seedling/stem. P3 (Black Pot) contains the rabbit anchor and a single leaf.

plant_audit.P1: Yellow pot shows dark, damp soil with no visible plant matter. Status: Dormant or Deceased.
plant_audit.P2_P4: Black pot contains a single, thin, green stem with a small leaf. Status: Critical/Stunted.
plant_audit.P3: Black pot contains one healthy-looking Pothos leaf and the white rabbit anchor. Status: Stable.

biome_observations.anomalies: Presence of crushed eggshell fragments in the P2/P4 pot (New Intervention). Presence of a multi-pin electronic component (header) on the desk surface near the pots.

temporal_deltas.T-5_to_T-3: Stable; minimal change in leaf posture.
temporal_deltas.T-2: Significant lighting drop; image underexposed, making visual confirmation difficult.
temporal_deltas.T-1_to_CURRENT: Recovery of lighting; the P2/P4 stem appears to have shifted slightly in orientation, suggesting potential phototropic response or manual adjustment.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is in a state of high stress. P1, P2, and P4 are effectively non-functional as botanical specimens. P3 is the only viable occupant. The presence of eggshells suggests an attempt at soil amendment or pest deterrence.
vision_report.anomalies: The introduction of electronic components (header pins) into the immediate desk environment is noted as a non-botanical intervention.
vision_report.narrative_description: The botanical collection is currently failing to thrive. The registry does not match the visual evidence, as three of the four registered plants are absent or dead. The remaining Pothos leaf in P3 is the only sign of life. The environment appears to be a laboratory or experimental desk setup rather than a traditional garden, given the presence of electronic debris.
